feedback.text: The concerns identified in the 2024 fitness check are clearly reflected in how digital products are often managed at the end of their lifecycle. When companies make purchased content inaccessible by shutting down services or revoking access, consumers lose both value and trust. This practice is comparable to the unfair tactics already flagged in the evaluation, such as manipulative design and misleading marketing, because it undermines informed choice and erodes confidence in the digital marketplace. Stronger enforcement and clearer obligations for long-term access would ensure consumers are treated fairly and that digital products uphold the same standards of reliability expected in traditional markets.
